title: Piumy status
description: Data contract between the core (switchboard) and the display adapter. The core writes it atomically; the display reads it and draws the face. The core owns mood + speech (it knows the events); the renderer maps mood -> a pool of face variants and animates within a mood without re-deriving meaning.

mood.description: Face family the renderer draws. The core sets it from events; transient moods (reading, switching, responding, thinking, done, new_msg, ai_online, vip) are set briefly (~4s) then revert to the queue-derived resting mood (zero/few/swamped) -- UNLESS a tier-3 system mood (error/qr/sleeping/muted/paused) is currently active, which transient events and the queue-derived resting mood can never override (see core's state.moodTier).

speech.description: Short chatter line shown under the face (pwnagotchi-style). The renderer may also rotate idle chatter on its own when this is empty.

queue.description: Advanced-mode messages waiting for an agent. Drives the QUEUE field and the resting mood bucket (0=zero, 1..N=few, >N=swamped).

last_msg.description: Short preview of the last inbound message (optional).

show_qr.description: Show the linking QR on screen (mood should be 'qr').

battery.description: Battery percentage, or null if unknown. The core is the ONLY writer of status.json: the power adapter writes its reading to a separate battery.json sidecar, which the core merges in here on a short heartbeat -- a stale or missing sidecar reading means null, never a placeholder. This is the LINEARIZED estimate (adapters/power/timeremain.py), not the raw voltage-curve percent -- the LiPo curve is non-linear (steep/flat/steep) and a raw-voltage percent looks jumpy even when accurate; see 'voltage' for the untouched real reading.

voltage.description: Real battery cell voltage in millivolts, or null if unknown. Same battery.json sidecar merge as 'battery' -- a stale/missing reading means null, never a placeholder.

charging.description: The power adapter's adaptive estimator (adapters/power/timeremain.py) inferred the battery is charging -- either the cell voltage is rising or the level has sat pinned at 100% for a while. Drives the charging-bolt battery icon.

time_remaining.description: Adaptive estimate of battery minutes remaining, or null if unknown. Starts theoretical (capacity/draw) and is replaced by the real learned discharge rate once enough samples exist -- see adapters/power/timeremain.py. While charging, this is the estimate IF unplugged right now, not a live countdown.

cpu.description: 1-minute load average scaled to 0..100 by core count, or null if unavailable (non-Linux dev host). Core-read from /proc/loadavg.

ram.description: Used-RAM percent ((MemTotal-MemAvailable)/MemTotal), or null if unavailable. Core-read from /proc/meminfo.

wifi.description: WiFi signal level 0..4 (0 = not connected), or null if unknown.

ip.description: Current LAN IP, or '' when offline.

hostname.description: mDNS/host name shown in the footer, e.g. 'pimywa.local'.

ssid.description: Current WiFi network name, or '' when unknown/not connected. Read via a generic Linux interface (`iw dev <iface> link`), never a vendor-specific lib.

agent_connected.description: True while at least one agent is connected over MCP. Mirrors agents > 0 — kept for older readers.

agents.description: Count of MCP sessions currently considered connected (a tool call within the last AgentIdle window). 0 means no agent connected.

uptime.description: Seconds since this core process started (not OS uptime).

reconnect_paused.description: The anti-ban governor paused reconnection (mood is 'paused' while this is true -- ban-risk signal, distinct from peaceful 'sleeping').

muted.description: The anti-ban mute/kill switch is on -- send_message is rejected while true. Mirrors the governor's kill switch into this contract (renamed from the earlier internal "killed" naming -- "mudo esta bien").

sent.description: All-time count of successfully sent outbound messages (pwnagotchi-style SENT n footer). Derived from message history, not a separately incremented counter.

face.description: The literal kaomoji string the display adapter's renderer drew for the current mood/animation frame, e.g. '(◕‿◕)'. The core is the ONLY writer of status.json: the display adapter writes its choice to a face.json sidecar, which the core merges in here on a short heartbeat -- a stale or missing sidecar reading means null, never a placeholder. Lets the dashboard mirror the e-paper's live face without duplicating the display adapter's face-selection catalog. null when the display adapter isn't running/hasn't rendered yet, or the current mood is 'qr' (no kaomoji face is drawn then).

updated_at.description: UTC RFC3339 timestamp of the last update.
